_comment: COMPLETE EXAMPLE - Multiple signals with OpenSignals metadata showing various trust levels and verification requirements
_disclaimer: This is a conceptual integration example. Not an official AdCP or OpenSignals specification.

signals.name: UK Premium Spirits Enthusiasts (Age 25+)
signals.description: First-party audience of UK spirits enthusiasts with age-verified profiles, 25+ only, interest in premium and craft spirits

trust_scores.provenance_reasoning: First-party data from authenticated beverage subscription and loyalty programs with documented collection methodology
trust_scores.permissioning_reasoning: Explicit opt-in consent for alcohol advertising with clear usage terms and age verification
trust_scores.freshness_reasoning: Updated within last 48 hours with daily refresh cycle
trust_scores.quality_reasoning: High coverage (68% of target demo) with validated precision through panel matching
trust_scores.explainability_reasoning: Full documentation with transparent methodology and segment composition details
trust_scores.outcome_reasoning: Validated performance in similar premium alcohol campaigns with 2.3x conversion uplift
trust_scores.compliance_reasoning: UK ASA compliant, age-gated, GDPR certified, no minors targeting

governance_requirements.approval_reason: UK alcohol advertising requires human review per brand policy and ASA regulations

signals.name: Premium Lifestyle Content - UK Publishers
signals.description: Page-level contextual targeting for premium lifestyle content including travel, fashion, dining, and luxury goods on verified UK publisher inventory

trust_scores.provenance_reasoning: Real-time NLP-based content analysis with transparent methodology and no third-party data
trust_scores.permissioning_reasoning: Consent not required - privacy-safe contextual analysis with no user tracking or profiling
trust_scores.freshness_reasoning: Real-time page analysis with sub-second refresh
trust_scores.quality_reasoning: High precision (94%) validated through publisher content audits and brand safety checks
trust_scores.explainability_reasoning: Fully transparent NLP model with keyword taxonomy and content classification details
trust_scores.outcome_reasoning: Demonstrated performance across luxury and premium brand campaigns
trust_scores.compliance_reasoning: Privacy-preserving by design, GDPR compliant, no PII processing, TAG certified

governance_requirements.approval_reason: Contextual signals can be autonomously approved for most categories including alcohol

signals.name: Modeled Affluent Consumer Segment
signals.description: Third-party modeled audience segment predicting affluent consumers based on aggregated behavioral and demographic indicators

trust_scores.provenance_reasoning: Third-party aggregated data from multiple sources with limited chain of custody documentation
trust_scores.permissioning_reasoning: Legitimate interest basis with some consent gaps; unclear revocation mechanism
trust_scores.freshness_reasoning: Updated weekly; some data points older than 30 days
trust_scores.quality_reasoning: Moderate coverage (52%) with modeled precision (not validated); no ground truth comparison
trust_scores.explainability_reasoning: Basic documentation available but modeling methodology is partially proprietary
trust_scores.outcome_reasoning: Self-reported performance data; no third-party validation
trust_scores.compliance_reasoning: GDPR compliant but limited transparency on data sources and consent chains

governance_requirements.approval_reason: Limited trust score (0.68) requires human review; third-party modeled data with provenance gaps

quality_concerns: Provenance score below 0.70 - limited source transparency
Quality score below 0.70 - unvalidated modeled data
Weekly refresh may be stale for fast-moving campaigns

signals.name: Brand-Safe News & Current Events Context
signals.description: Brand-safe news and current events content on verified premium news publishers with real-time content classification

trust_scores.provenance_reasoning: First-party content analysis by accredited news publishers with transparent methodology
trust_scores.permissioning_reasoning: Consent not required - contextual content classification with no user tracking
trust_scores.freshness_reasoning: Real-time content classification with continuous monitoring
trust_scores.quality_reasoning: High precision brand safety classification; MRC-accredited verification
trust_scores.explainability_reasoning: Full transparency on content classification taxonomy and brand safety criteria
trust_scores.outcome_reasoning: Proven brand safety track record with zero adjacency incidents in 12 months
trust_scores.compliance_reasoning: GDPR compliant, TAG certified, MRC accredited brand safety measurement

governance_requirements.approval_reason: Contextual brand safety signal suitable for autonomous activation

signals.name: Unverified Consumer Interest Segment
signals.description: Low-cost consumer interest segment with limited documentation and unverified provenance

trust_scores.provenance_reasoning: Unknown data sources, no documented chain of custody, third-party aggregated with no verification
trust_scores.permissioning_reasoning: Unclear consent mechanism, no documented legitimate interest basis, high revocation risk
trust_scores.freshness_reasoning: Last updated 45 days ago with irregular update schedule
trust_scores.quality_reasoning: Low coverage (28%), no precision validation, no accuracy metrics provided
trust_scores.explainability_reasoning: Minimal documentation, proprietary methodology with no transparency
trust_scores.outcome_reasoning: No performance data available, no historical validation
trust_scores.compliance_reasoning: Basic GDPR compliance claimed but no audit trail or certifications

governance_requirements.approval_reason: Low trust score (0.38) - MUST NOT activate without explicit human override for all categories

quality_concerns: CRITICAL: Provenance score 0.28 - unknown data sources and collection methods
CRITICAL: Quality score 0.33 - unvalidated with no accuracy metrics
HIGH: Permissioning score 0.35 - unclear consent and high compliance risk
MEDIUM: Stale data - last updated 45 days ago
RECOMMENDATION: DO NOT ACTIVATE - Signal does not meet minimum trust requirements for any use case
open_signals.warning: This signal has a LOW TRUST rating and should NOT be activated without explicit approval and risk acceptance from brand policy owner. Not suitable for regulated categories including alcohol.

recommended_workflow.step_1: Filter signals by trust_band - consider only 'highly_trusted' and 'trusted' for alcohol campaigns
recommended_workflow.step_2: For each candidate signal, call OpenSignals verify_signal with brand=premium-spirits-co and category=alcohol
recommended_workflow.step_3: For signals requiring human approval, initiate approval workflow via governance agent
recommended_workflow.step_4: For approved signals, call OpenSignals bind_signal_policy to attach alcohol-specific policies
recommended_workflow.step_5: Call AdCP activate_signal for policy-bound approved signals
recommended_workflow.step_6: Call OpenSignals audit_signal_usage after campaign activation

integration_notes.trust_level_filtering: Buyer agents should filter by trust_band before detailed verification to improve efficiency
integration_notes.human_approval_optimization: Signals with decision_mode='approve_with_human' should be batched for human review
integration_notes.alcohol_compliance: All alcohol campaigns must verify age restrictions, geographic compliance, and ASA/Portman Group adherence
integration_notes.contextual_advantages: Contextual signals typically have higher trust scores and lower governance requirements due to privacy-safe nature
integration_notes.quality_thresholds: Consider setting minimum trust score thresholds (e.g., 0.75 for alcohol campaigns) in brand policy
